claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child in care for 16 weeks but spends 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To get started, could you tell me: **How many children are you responsible for, and what are their names?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm responsible for 2 children - Alex, who is 18, and Blake, who is 13.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. So you have two children: Alex (18) and Blake (13).

Let me ask you a few questions to assess your eligibility for each of them.

**For Alex, who is 18:** Is Alex currently in approved education or training (such as school, college, university, or an apprenticeship)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in education or training at the moment. They finished school and aren't doing any courses right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

**For Blake, who is 13:** Do you live with Blake, or are you paying towards their upkeep (such as food, clothes, or pocket money)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Blake lives with their other parent, actually. They're the one who looks after Blake day-to-day.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. That's helpful to know.

**Regarding Blake:** Are you making any financial contributions towards Blake's upkeep (such as food, clothes, pocket money, or housing costs), even though they live with their other parent?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not currently contributing financially towards Blake.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me confirm a couple of details to make sure I have the full picture.

**Regarding your own circumstances:** Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK at the moment - I live abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

Before I provide my final assessment, I need to ask one more question:

**Are you a Crown servant, or do you have a right to reside in the country where you're currently living that would allow you to claim Child Benefit while living abroad?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant and I don't have any special right to reside that would allow me to claim Child Benefit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Let me explain the outcome:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Summary:**

Unfortunately, you are **not eligible for Child Benefit** for either Alex or Blake:

- **Alex:** At 18 years old and not in approved education or training, you cannot claim Child Benefit for them.

- **Blake:** Although Blake is 13 (under 16), they live with their other parent and you are not making financial contributions towards their upkeep. To be eligible, you would need to either live with Blake or contribute financially to their care.

Additionally, since you don't live in the UK and you're not a Crown servant or covered by special residency arrangements, this further prevents you from claiming Child Benefit.

If your circumstances change—for example, if you move back to the UK, Alex enters education or training, or you start contributing financially to Blake's upkeep—you may become eligible. You can contact HM Revenue and Customs (HMRC) if you'd like to discuss your situation further.

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old. You can only get Child Benefit for a child aged 16 or over if they are in approved education or training. You confirmed that Alex is not currently in education or training, so you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 13 years old and lives with their other parent. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you live with the child or if you're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards looking after them. You confirmed that you are not making any financial contributions towards Blake's upkeep. Therefore, you do not meet the criteria for eligibility for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are not eligible for Child Benefit for either child. Additionally, you do not live in the UK, and you are not a Crown servant or covered by special residency arrangements that would allow you to claim Child Benefit from abroad. To be eligible for Child Benefit, you would typically need to live in the UK and be responsible for a child (either by living with them or contributing financially to their upkeep). Neither of these conditions is currently met in your situation.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 18 years old. You can only get Child Benefit for a child aged 16 or over if they are in approved education or training. You confirmed that Alex is not currently in education or training, so you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 13 years old and lives with their other parent. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you live with the child or if you're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards looking after them. You confirmed that you are not making any financial contributions towards Blake's upkeep. Therefore, you do not meet the criteria for eligibility for Blake.

response.overall_reasoning: You are not eligible for Child Benefit for either child. Additionally, you do not live in the UK, and you are not a Crown servant or covered by special residency arrangements that would allow you to claim Child Benefit from abroad. To be eligible for Child Benefit, you would typically need to live in the UK and be responsible for a child (either by living with them or contributing financially to their upkeep). Neither of these conditions is currently met in your situation.
